name: AI/ML UX Patterns
description: User experience patterns for AI and machine learning powered features

transparency.description: Users should understand AI is involved and how it works
practices: Provide 'Why did I see this?' option

control.description: Users should control AI, not be controlled by it

trust.description: Build confidence through reliability and honesty

ux_patterns.explain_why: Show reason ('Because you watched X' or 'Based on your history')
ux_patterns.diversity: Don't just show similar - introduce discovery

ux_patterns.ghost_text: Show suggestion in gray, Tab to accept
ux_patterns.non_intrusive: Don't force acceptance, easy to ignore

ux_patterns.confidence_levels: Show confidence (90% sure it's a cat)
ux_patterns.fallback: Provide manual option if AI fails

ux_patterns.set_expectations: Clarify it's a bot, explain capabilities

ux_considerations.filter_bubble: Expose users to diverse content, not just personalized

loading_states.ai_processing: AI is analyzing... (show progress if possible)
loading_states.longer_waits: AI tasks may take seconds-minutes, show estimates
loading_states.skeleton_screens: Show placeholder while AI generates content

confidence_indicators.description: Show how confident AI is in its prediction
confidence_indicators.use_when: Predictions that users will act on (medical, financial)

techniques.feature_importance: This decision was based on: age (40%), location (30%), history (30%)
techniques.similar_examples: This is like cases A, B, C
techniques.counterfactuals: If X changed to Y, decision would be different

feedback_loops.benefit: AI improves over time, users feel in control

patterns: Show uncertainty and ask for help

bias.issue: AI can perpetuate or amplify biases in training data
examples: Facial recognition worse for dark skin

privacy.issue: AI often requires large amounts of user data

filter_bubbles.mitigation: Expose users to diverse content, show 'Outside your usual' sections

approaches.unit_tests: Test AI model accuracy with test datasets
approaches.ux_testing: Observe users interacting with AI features
approaches.monitoring: Track AI accuracy, user corrections, escalations in production
approaches.red_teaming: Deliberately try to break AI (adversarial testing)

best_practices: Allow users to correct AI mistakes
Explain AI decisions in plain language
Let users opt-out of AI features
Design for AI failures (they will happen)
Don't hide AI errors (acknowledge and explain)
Escalate to humans when AI is stuck
Test with diverse users and data
Show progress for long AI tasks
anti_patterns: No way to correct AI mistakes
Annoying AI suggestions (smart replies after every message)

good.spotify_discover_weekly: Personalized playlist with 'Why this song?' explanations
good.gmail_smart_compose: Non-intrusive suggestions, easy to ignore, learns from rejections
good.netflix_recommendations: Shows reason ('Because you watched X'), allows feedback

bad.amazon_alexa_random_laughs: AI bug caused random laughing (creepy, no explanation)
bad.microsoft_tay: Chatbot learned offensive language from users (no moderation)
bad.apple_card_credit_limits: Alleged gender bias in credit limits (black box AI, no explanation)
